message:
fix(validator): harmonize with actual ACFS checksums.yaml format

The validator warned about "missing version" for every installer, but
the ACFS checksums.yaml format doesn't include version fields — only
url and sha256. This was noise, not a useful signal.

Changes:
- Validator now checks for missing sha256 instead of missing version,
  since sha256 is the field that actually matters in the ACFS format
- Replaced MissingVersion error variant with MissingChecksum
- List command: shows "sha256" indicator instead of "(vunknown)"
- Dry-run output: omits version entirely when absent instead of
  showing "(vunknown)"
- JSON/JSONL output: includes url and sha256 fields instead of version
- Updated test_validate_missing_version_warns to
  test_validate_missing_sha256_warns

Result: `validate` now reports "checksums.yaml is valid" with zero
warnings for the actual ACFS checksums.yaml (all 41 entries have
both url and sha256).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
